rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues to be cleared: 1. Petition to include what relief being sought. 2. Petition must be verified by the fiduciary. Prob. Code sec. 1023. This petition was verified by Counsel. 3. Notice of Hearing to be given to all parties who are entitled to. Prob. Code sec. 12201(b). CRC 7.53. 4. Proposed Order must be submitted prior to the hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. 5. Information re: if there is a change in finances Payment of the court filing fees as Petition alleges the estate is solvent and the applicant has not reported any change in his/her finances. The court to review fee waiver. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DEL PRADO FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Restoration Of Co-Trustee And Beneficiary Rene Del Prado To The Del Prado Family Trust And For Removal Of Court Appointed Trustee Bennett Hong, Along With Denial Of Trustee Fees For Neglect And Mismanagement Of Trust Property And Assets And Other Trust Violations. Demand For Full Accounting On Proceeds Of Unlawful Property Sale And For Reversal Of Property Sale And Damages Attributable To Trustees Overall Malfeasance
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/27/2023 per petitioner (in pro per)'s request. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The petition, first supplement, and third supplement must be verified with language required by CCP section 2015.5. 3) The petition must state the names and addresses of each person entitled to notice. See Probate Code section 17201. 4) The proof of service attached to the DE-120 form filed on 08/08/2023 shows notice was given less than 30 days before this hearing. New 30-day notice of the continued hearing must be given.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN PANARELLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress, as the following issues remain unaddressed by the petitioner: 1) The box at Item 1 of the DE-120 form must be unchecked, as no status report has been filed. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information attached to the DE-120 form must also include the CourtCall appearance information. 3) A copy of the decedent's death certificate shows 03/28/2021 as the date of death (not 03/20/2021). 4) As to the surviving issue of the decedent's maternal grandparents: i) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) to locate Charles Steven Madrid's contact information must be filed. ii) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) to locate Zachary Madrid's contact information must be filed. iii) If Benjamin Sandoval Jr. is incarcerated, notice under Probate Code section 9202(b) must be given. iv) No proof of heirship has been filed for Cordelia Womack. v) A certified copy of a spousal property order or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of David Lopez' death certificate must be filed to determine Mary Cooley's entitlement to distribution. vi) Frank Lopez Jr. appears to have survived the decedent. A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative of his estate must be filed, or an original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of his death certificate must be filed to determine Terrance Lopez/Kevin Lopez' entitlement to distribution. vii) Please provide a copy of Denise Lopez' birth certificate or other proof of her child-parent relationship with Frank Lopez. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEFFREY ALAN MEYERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Termination Of Proceedings And Discharge
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to be mailed to all persons who filed Request for Special Notice. Prob. Code sec. 12201(b) and 1220(a)(2)(B). 2) Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to be mailed to Amy Harrington. Proof of service of Electronic Service on file, showing email service on 7/10/23; however, it is unknown if Amy Harrington has expressly consented on e-service. Prob. Code sec. 1215(c). 3) Petition is brought under the statue of Texas, not under the California statue while the administration has been subject to the law of California alleging that the decedent was a resident of California. 4) Petition does not set forth under which authority the court can grant the request not to allow any (statutory) compensation to the former attorney for the fiduciaries. 5) Proposed Order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALZINA MARIE RAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Distribute Share Of Estate Of Heir Marcus Mcclure To Special Needs Trust For His Benefit
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/18/2023. If there is no conservatorship case, a petition to establish a special needs trust must be filed in a new trust case which must be opened under the name of the proposed trust. The following issues must also be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. A copy of the proposed trust must be attached to the order. LRSF 14.48(B). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) No inventory and appraisal is on file. Please provide a specific description and value of each asset distributable to Marcus McClure from this estate, together with annual income from each asset, for the purpose of setting the trustee's initial bond. 4) No guardian ad litem has been appointed for Marcus McClure. Does he qualify as a person who lacks legal capacity to make decisions within the meaning of Probate Code section 1003(a)(2)? 5) A budget of anticipated expenditures to be paid out of the proposed trust must be provided. LRSF 14.49(B). 6) Please identify all public benefits received by Marcus McClure. 7) Please identify issue of Marcus McClure. If he has no issue, please identify his heirs. 8) Does the petitioner, if appointed as trustee, intend to carry on the day-to-day activity of the trust in the county where the petitioner resides? If so, should the Court transfer jurisdiction to the county of the petitioner's residence per section 3604(a)(1)?
